summary_one_line: Input must begin with the 3-byte UTF-8 BOM literal 0xEF 0xBB 0xBF at offsets 0..2 (with len>=3).

hypotheses.what_input_feature: First three input bytes must equal the UTF-8 BOM literal 0xEF, 0xBB, 0xBF (checked by a chain of equality comparisons against constants in xmlDetectCharEncoding at encoding.c:987-988), with len>=3.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: cmplog intercepts the byte-CMPs against 0xEF/0xBB/0xBF and feeds those literals to I2SRandReplace, which substitutes the constants at the matching input offsets in one mutation step; cmplog resolves the branch 10/10 trials (avg 227 hits on the true side).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive has no CMP introspection — it can only land all three magic bytes by chance via havoc byte mutations (probability ~1/2^24 per attempt), so it blocks 9/10 trials with zero hits on the true side at line 987.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace in cmplog reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the captured CMP operands (0xEF, 0xBB, 0xBF) into the input at locations matching the other operand; an I2S-floor (dash) row at depth 4 of a winning seed's lineage corroborates I2S participation in the chain that produced the resolving seed.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a 3-way equality test against the UTF-8 BOM bytes 0xEF, 0xBB, 0xBF at encoding.c:987-988.
The branch is guarded by a len>=3 precondition.
cmplog resolves this branch in all 10 trials while naive blocks in 9 of 10 trials, the decisive I2S divergence at this branch.
Winner trial counts show cmplog resolved=10/10 vs loser naive blocked=9/10 with hit-count divergence of 227 on the branch.
On the true side at line 987 (winning side), W traces show T=40 while L traces show T=0, i.e. the loser never takes the BOM-matching side.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes captured CMP operands directly into the input at matching offsets, enabling one-step magic-byte synthesis.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no CMP introspection and can only flip bytes blindly via havoc.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 2243 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 039dbbb1fc96a2e4`. The 5-level ancestor chain contains 1 I2S-floor (dash) row at depth 4 (ancestor 8c7bd7ba6dfb824c with mutation_op = -), characteristic of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in cmplog under the current LibAFL build (lower-bound signal per the cmplog mechanism note).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A parameterized synthetic with a 3-byte equality gate against a fixed magic literal that nonetheless shows cmplog NOT outperforming naive (e.g. cmplog resolves <=naive in trial counts at the same duration) — that would refute the claim that I2SRandReplace is the mechanism flipping this branch.

weakest_evidence_point: Only one of five winning seeds was checked for lineage; the I2S-floor signal at depth 4 confirms I2S participated somewhere in this seed's chain but does not prove the specific dash-row ancestor is what placed the 0xEF/0xBB/0xBF triple — havoc steps after that ancestor could have rearranged the bytes.
